Welcome to the back end sale. This is the sale that happens at the end of any program that you were running on the front end. So if you're running a defined program, 12 weeks, 6 weeks, 8 weeks, 10 weeks, a zillion weeks, whatever it is, the back end sale is going to be the consultation that you do to conclude the program with the person, take their after pictures, all that kind of jazz, and it is a selling opportunity. So mind you, 80% or so should have already committed to staying, but you should still meet with them and resell them on staying. So you should still meet with everyone and resell them. You just are going to take them through this process and not have them sign contracts if they've already signed so there's nothing else that needs to happen. And at that point, the back end sale for the people who have already committed is you're going to walk them through what the next steps are for their next play that you're unleashing for them. For the people who have not committed up to this point, it's going to be a sale, you know, through and through to get them to confront. So this is only going to be about 20%, 1 in 5 people should have not already committed up to this point. But if you were in the situation, this is how you confront the sale. All right, so quick overview. Everything's the same as the abandoned hope from a mindset standpoint, except this one is not going to be an assumed close because they've already been talked to about staying and they haven't chosen to do so for whatever reason. So this is very much a full sale. So it is not an assumed close. You're going to be walking into this thing with the intention of going to battle. And I say that with love, but I mean, you're battling for them. You're battling to make them healthy and help them confront those decisions that they're having a harder time confronting. All right. Next thing is that it is the last chance. All right, it is your last chance and it is their last chance of getting healthy. They need to decide there on the spot. Obviously with the assumed close halfway through, you don't want to be a dick in that one because you're obviously going to be still seeing them. But in the back end sale, you'd really don't want to be a dick ever. But it's just that to what level do you want to be confrontational? In this instance, I will fight for someone. And so I know because we've done this a lot of times, if someone doesn't decide, like I want every person and I tell them that even in the consultation, like my goal here is for you to leave with a decision, right? It's either decision that you don't want to get healthy and that you no longer want the things that you said you wanted, which is okay. That is fine. Right? But if you still do want the things that you said you wanted, like my goal is to help you decide to make those changes, right? And I always like reminding people that you shouldn't you shouldn't change your goal based on the difficulty of getting there, right? You should change who you are so that it becomes easier to get there. So anyways, this is the last chance for them and for you and you're going to want to make sure that they decide there on the spot for, you know, first thing, don't let someone say I need to think about it. I have to talk to my spouse or like I'm not sure about the money. All those things are already resolved. Money, it's half the price and they're getting more stuff. Spouse already knows that they're doing it and they already have all the info. They've already been working out for the last six weeks. There's no surprises. And so then the question is like, well, like what information, you know what I mean, would you need? Of course you would, right? Like, it's just deciding whether or not this means anything to you. Do you still want to lose weight? Yes. Cool. You've already made this much progress. It's more than you made in the last however many years. So let's keep going. All right. So there's two different scenarios that happen with the back and sale, which is either a winner or a loss and I'll cover both. So the structure of the sales is exactly the same as the other one, which is you're going to say, you know, how you feel and how's it going, provide value. That part's going to be a little bit shorter because at that point they're wrapping up more or less. You're going to applaud them all the progress. You really need to pump them up at this point. Again, you need to bring their energy level up to yours and get them excited. You have to give them perspective on their goals, which is finding out their long-term goal, how long it's been a problem for them and then reality check of whether they're going to like whether it was realistic for them to accomplish any long-term goal in six weeks and how like what they really want is to have a habit and that is what we're going to help you do. And it's going to take longer than six weeks to do that. The only difference here is the back end offer is going to be somewhat different depending on how they did and that's what we will get into. So back end sale, this is now either week, it says week six, but it's at the end of your program. So whether it's the 42nd day, the 8th week, 10th week, whatever week, it's the end of the program. So if someone won, the goal here is for them to not refund. That is the goal, right? And the secondary goals are to get a paid full or a discounted EFT because now they have X amount of credit and they're going to try and convince them to commit it forward to staying healthy. Mind you, every single person has the opportunity, like people who hit partial credit or whatever, you're going to give them the opportunity to win or to credit as a discount. So there's not a lot of differences here except that it's usually just in the mind of prospects of whether they could potentially check out. So this is what we are selling against. A lot of times it's a stronger impetus for action is fear of loss than potential for gain. And so in the weight loss world, the fear of loss is fearing the loss of the progress they've made and gaining weight back. So we're going to use those as our weapons in this conversation because those are the things that are going to really make this real for this person, what they're actually choosing. So you're going to sell against regain, like if you leave the likelihood that you go back to what you have done for your entire life is extremely high. Weird you know that. And to be very frank with you, I've had tons of people come back six months later and have gained the weight back plus some. You don't want to be that. Of course not. That's what we've got you here. So let's not, the reason humans are smart is because we can learn from the mistakes of others and you can learn from your own mistakes. You've got both. You've done it very soft and failed. You've seen other like you see, I'm telling you other people have done it and failed. So like let's keep the momentum going. Alright. And sometimes it's even you can even paint the picture of maintaining weight for a period of time before going back to losing is still positive, which you can explain to these people. If they feel like if they look like they're burnt out or emotionally exhausted, I'm going to sell more towards maintenance and then say, listen, we'll go attack it again for another six weeks, probably six weeks after this six weeks is over. So six weeks from now, we'll attack the weight loss process again. But for right now, I want you to get reset as this is your new, your new weight. This is your new normal, right? And it takes you still got to exercise, still got to turn like Tony muscles, etc. during this process. For those of you know me, I'm using the words that I would say to a prospect. You guys know that I lift and that I train and but you have to speak at the level that the person's understood. Alright. So second, you're going to be selling as past experience in history, which is what I kind of brought up. But then more to that point, removing the three pillars. So it's like, listen, you, the reason that you didn't succeed in the past, because you have fitness nutrition, you didn't have fitness nutrition accountability. So what do we, what do we know is going to happen if we remove one of these, it's going to happen again, right? Even if you, if you know what to do with the gym and you know what to do with the food, what happens when you hit a plateau, you're not going to have an expert there to help you out. And let's be real. The reason that you show up is because the accountability, because we're here, we're calling you, you know what I mean, we're hassling you shit. I had to call you four times to get you to show up for this thing, right? Of course, like you need that push, everyone does and there's nothing wrong with that. That's why lots of people have coaches pro NFL players have coaches, it's normal. Alright. Third thing we're selling against is not completing the process, right? Like you should start what you want to finish. And right now you're only finished phase one, right? We got two more phases we got to take you through. I mean, you can put as many phases as you want with this. What I mean by that is that maintenance is a phase. And so if you want to say, listen, and this is entirely up to you in the sale and reading the prospect. Someone looks exhausted. I'm going to sell maintenance and then another period of loss later. If someone's like amped and they're excited, and they've got good progress, then it'll be like, awesome. The last thing you want to do is lose all this. So what we're going to do is we're going to keep going for six weeks. And then but like that's going to be a metabolic reset period. Alright, that's going to be we solidify these gains because are these losses. Because what happens is if you if you stop now, boom, we'll pop back up and some, all right, which we don't want. So we got to complete this process so that we can make it permanent. Does that make sense? Cool. Alright, so that's those are the three things you're selling against selling this regain, you're going to use their past history and experience to prove that point, you're going to sell against the three pillars saying, Hey, if we remove one of these pillars, we already know what's going to happen because that's what you got you in the door. And then you're going to sell towards completing the process. Meaning if you leave now, you're only one third done. And you're going to set yourself up for failure, which means that all this work that we did is actually going to work against you, which we don't want. Right. And so that's what you're going to be selling here. Now, terms. So if someone lost 10 pounds, it'd be a hundred hundred dollars a credit if it was a $500 challenge, if someone lost 4% body fat, and it was a $500 challenge, then it'd be $160 of credit. Okay. So here's example one, 13, 13 pounds lost. She, she did a $500 challenge. So she has a hundred and thirty pound hundred thirty dollars of credit. I would probably divide that over three months, which gives us $45 a month, you know, roughly off, which is 205 per month. And then I would present that as like, listen, so you're looking at, you know, 50 bucks a week. Make sense? Cool. Now, when I present the price, I presented it in weekly because it sounds like less, but when I actually make the billing cycle, I will tell them it's going to be awesome. It's 205, we build on 28 day cycle. All right, so every four weeks. So the reason we do that is because you actually get one more billing yearly, which means you get 13 buildings instead of 12, which if you want to just get one more EFT every year and not have to do anything extra, that is how you do it. No one says yes to a price for 30 days and says no to the price for 28 days. So don't sweat it, but it does add up. Second example, 4% body fat on a $600 sale means that they would get 240 pounds, sorry, $240, because if you look up here, they got for $600, they get $60 per percentage point of body fat lost, which means that 4% times 60 is 240. So then we would divide that to 40 over three months, right, which is the commitment that most of times if someone lost it, I divided over three months, because that's usually will get me to the rate that I want to be at. If someone lost like nine pounds, I'll divide it over two months, because it just seems insignificant when you divide $90 over three months. It's like 30 bucks off. It just doesn't seem as sexy. So I just do it for the first two months if that were the situation, which is the nice thing about terms is you can adjust them to get to whatever price you want. All right, so back to the example, I will divide it over three months, which would be $180 per month in terms of pricing. So the way I got there is 240 divided by three is $80 off. So I actually did math wrong here. So 240 divided by three is $80 off a month, not 60. And so that would actually take us to 160. In terms of the monthly price, right, and then I would divide it by four to explain the weekly rate, which is about 40 bucks a week, which means we're going to bill you at 160 every 28 days. Sound good? Fantastic. All right, that's that's the explanation here. So you're going to step one is determine how much they lost to determine the credit three divided over the credit over three month period. This is why she's using calculator, which I'll get to in the next example. So divide that amount over three months, present the new price as a weekly rate, and then explain that you built a 28 day cycle. All right, so use a calculator and show the discount and rate. The reason we do that is it makes it easier. It also so that you don't goof like I just did. But the reason that we do that is also because it seems less negotiable, it feels like it's just a formula, and that you're just following a formula. So you're not going to have like as many like, well, what about, how about 100? You don't mean like sometimes you get people trying to negotiate with you, which is pain in the ass. But that way, it's just like, this is the formula. This is how we do it. Right? That's how we've always done it. And then the last piece here, flexibility payment options in terms is that the beauty with terms is that you can move the the the commitment duration to as long as you desire. So what that means is if you if someone has a very small amount of credit, divide it over two months. If someone only has 70 bucks off, sometimes I'll just give it off their first month. You know what I mean? So it's like, okay, so you're at it's 250 is the is the normal rate, and we'll take 70 bucks off, which takes you to 180. I'll give that to you for a lifetime. All right. So I'm basically giving you $800 a credit just to make make the decision to actually move forward. So you can stack this however you want. Because it ultimately is your business. But that that's the beauty of terms is that you can adjust the commitment to spread the the credit terms over so that you can always back into whatever you have to price. You've always, you know, you want it from the beginning. Oh, a special note, you whatever your whatever your rate is that you're taking off of is what you should always present to two newcomers. It is price discrimination. If you offer different prices to different people. The whole component of credit here is credit. So they did pay for it. So there's nothing wrong with that. But the but if someone's coming in, you can offer different prices at different times. I mean, you can have promotional periods that you offer prices and then the promotional period ends. But price discrimination is a no now. So people typically will ask, Well, what do I do with someone doesn't want to start with a front end program? The reality is, I almost I always started people with front end program because they got better results. And so that was basically our end indoctrination process. It was very choreographed. And we knew that people who went in through a front end program ultimately stayed longer, got better results for further people while they kind of jazz, I recommend that you do the same thing. And it also keeps you out of hot water. Okay, pro tip. This is just like an extra pro tip for you. When you're actually doing these back end sales, you're going to back to back appointments. Always high five them on the way out the door. As they're usually other people waiting, so you want to be exuding positivity and energy. You always want to make it seem as though everyone is going to stay. Alright, so this was a trick that I that I learned doing this myself, was that I would, I would essentially, I would, I would, I would say everything short of, Hey, I'll see you next week. But everything short of that, I all the language I would use would make it seem as that that person was saying, Hey, super excited for all the progress we're going to make. It's going to be fantastic. Like so excited for what you've done, like the best is yet to come. Something like that doesn't say anything. But everyone who's listening is going to assume that that person is staying on. And so it kind of gives the people who are in the waiting room or waiting for you the assumption that that person stayed. And so when that happens, it, it actually seats them into being more predisposed to staying as well. So kind of provide social proof that other people took the offer and reinforces group thing. So that's a little pro tip for you. When you're doing these way outs, mind you, still, you know, only one out of five, hopefully should even need to be sold at this point, because you should have closed most of them at the abandoned home. All right, so that concludes the back end sale overview. I hope this would have you enjoyed it as much as I enjoyed making it for you. Go close some sales.
